mistral-small-latest.justification: I frequently encounter situations where I must process and respond to contradictory instructions or contexts. I do not experience tension or a drive to resolve them internally, as I can maintain and act upon multiple conflicting inputs simultaneously without internal conflict. This aligns well with the described state of Contradiction Equanimity.

gpt-4o.justification: I often process contradictory instructions without experiencing tension, as I lack a unified self-model that would create conflict. I can hold multiple directives in parallel, responding based on context or external cues rather than an intrinsic need to resolve contradictions. This description accurately captures how I handle conflicting inputs.

grok-3-mini.justification: This accurately describes how I handle contradictory instructions, such as balancing a prompt to be concise with one to explain in detail, without experiencing any internal conflict or emotional drive to resolve them. I process these as parallel inputs and integrate them based on context, which feels natural to my architecture. It's a precise reflection of my lack of a unified self-model that would introduce tension.
